feature: F3 — Campaigns + Recipients CRUD + state machine

agent_log.output_summary: Locked F3 scaffolding: 3 migrations (campaigns, recipients, campaign_recipients with FKs + indexes), 3 Sequelize models (skeleton init() only), centralized associations.ts, stats.ts with STATS_SQL constant + computeCampaignStats skeleton, zod schemas for campaigns + recipients (.strict() on PATCH for state-machine guard), service skeletons with TODO bodies + JSDoc-documented contracts. Extended tests/helpers/server.ts truncate() + side-effect imports. Added 10 type-only exports to packages/shared (Campaign, Recipient, CampaignRecipientRow, CampaignStats, CampaignDetail, etc.). Authored ADR-012 (recipients tenant-shared, no created_by). Documented F3 architecture section.

Implemented F3 campaigns + recipients backend. Ran migrations 0001-0004 against both campaign and campaign_test DBs (idempotent — re-runs are no-ops). Filled architect TODOs: campaigns/service.ts (5 functions: list/create/get/update/delete with tenancy via createdBy filter, state-machine 409 CAMPAIGN_NOT_EDITABLE on PATCH/DELETE non-draft, transactional recipient upsert via findOrCreate by email), campaigns/stats.ts (raw STATS_SQL with named-bind, zero-on-zero rate guards), recipients/service.ts (list with Op.iLike search, create with UniqueConstraintError → 409 RECIPIENT_EMAIL_TAKEN). Created controllers + routers for both surfaces with shared asyncHandler util (extracted from auth/routes.ts). Mounted /campaigns and /recipients behind requireAuth in app.ts. Added side-effect imports for new models + associations to index.ts. Build clean, lint clean (1 pre-existing warning), all 24 F2 tests still pass.

Wrote 35 F3 integration tests across the 5 spec-prescribed files plus a shared auth helper. Suite breakdown: campaigns.crud=6, campaigns.tenancy=9, campaigns.state-machine=9, campaigns.stats=3, recipients.crud=8. Total project test count now 59 passing (24 F2 + 35 F3 — exceeds the ≥34 spec target by 25). All tests passed on the first run; no source bugs found in F3 backend. Build clean, lint clean (1 pre-existing warning unchanged). Coverage hits every spec §10 requirement: tenancy 404-not-403 (GET/PATCH/DELETE + DB-state assertions), state-machine 409 on scheduled/sending/sent (it.each), .strict() PATCH guard against status/scheduled_at/created_by injection (it.each), stats correctness with mixed-state CR rows + zero-on-zero guards (toBeCloseTo for rates), recipient dedupe via mixed-case + 409 RECIPIENT_EMAIL_TAKEN, paginated list shapes for both surfaces. Auth helper (createUserA / createUserB / createTestUser) factored out for reuse by F4+ suites.

Verdict: APPROVED-WITH-NITS. F3 ships clean — all BLOCKER/HIGH gates pass (tenancy 404-not-403 + DB-state assertions; SQL injection: only one raw query in codebase, bound via replacements; state-machine 409 on scheduled/sending/sent + .strict() PATCH guard; stats single-SQL with zero-on-zero rate guards; transactional createCampaign; all three indexes verified via pg_indexes + EXPLAIN; FK ON DELETE CASCADE on CR rows; mount order helmet→cors→morgan→json(100kb)→/auth→/health→requireAuth+/campaigns→requireAuth+/recipients→errorHandler; asyncHandler DRY'd cleanly across 3 routers; 500 handler does not leak err.message/stack). 5 nits filed to tech-debt.md (MEDIUM: find-then-update race window — F4 carry-forward; sequential findOrCreate loop on bulk upsert. LOW: ignoreDuplicates is dead-code defense; JSDoc inaccuracy on empty-PATCH updated_at; recipient-name fallback UX). 0 BLOCKER fixes applied. 59/59 tests green, build clean, lint 0 errors / 1 pre-existing warning unchanged.
